# Sagewai

> Sagewai is the autonomous agent platform: describe the goal, we design the agents, run them in production, and fine-tune local models so every run gets cheaper.

Sagewai is open-source software for building and running AI agents in production. You write an agent in a few lines of Python, give it tools and memory, and run it on your own machine or as a fleet of workers on your own hardware. One interface reaches 100+ models via LiteLLM, so you are not locked to a provider. Sagewai is early software — the SDK is published as 0.1.1 (alpha); the claim notes below say what ships today versus what is on the roadmap.

## What you get

- **SDK** — Python-native agent runtime. 100+ models via LiteLLM, MCP tools, typed memory, guardrails, multi-step workflows. https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/core-concepts/agents
- **Autopilot** — Describe the goal in plain English and Sagewai assembles and runs the agent. Linear plans run today; branched plans and automatic healing are in progress. https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/platform/autopilot
- **Fleet** — Distributed workers, capability-based dispatch, project isolation, sandboxed execution (Docker, Kubernetes). https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/platform/fleet
- **Observatory** — OpenTelemetry, VictoriaMetrics, Grafana dashboards, per-model and per-team cost tracking. Answer "what did AI cost us?" in one place. https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/platform/observatory
- **Training Loop** — Capture good production runs and fine-tune a local model from them. Run capture ships today; the end-to-end fine-tune-and-deploy loop is on the v1.1 roadmap. https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/platform/training-loop

**Sealed** is the security layer: per-workload identity, an external secret backend (HashiCorp Vault), and admin controls ship today; runtime enforcement (live secret injection, redaction, per-key ACL, mid-run revocation) is maturing. https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/platform/security

## Quickstart

- Install + first agent: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/get-started/quickstart
- Minimal setup (no sandbox required): https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/get-started/minimal-setup
- Build your first agent: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/get-started/first-agent
- Architecture overview: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/architecture

## Use cases

End-to-end walkthroughs — each ships with a README and a runnable demo.

- Customer support triage agent: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/core-concepts/agents
- Train your own model: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/guides/training
- See where the AI spend goes: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/platform/observatory
- Multi-tenant production with isolated tenants: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/tutorials/production-multitenancy
- Long-context conversations on local LLMs: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/core-concepts/memory
- Graph memory for multi-hop retrieval: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/core-concepts/memory
- Credential isolation with sandbox + Sealed: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/guides/sealed-vault

## Architecture

The platform is a control plane plus a worker fleet. The control plane plans, persists, and dispatches; the fleet executes. Every workflow run flows through this split.

- Architecture overview: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/architecture
- Runtime topology: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/architecture/runtime-topology
- Security tiers (Tier-1, Tier-2, Tier-3): https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/architecture/security-tiers
- Execution modes: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/architecture/execution-modes
- Sandbox backends (Docker, Kubernetes, Null): https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/architecture/sandbox-backends

## Inference cost-down spectrum

Sagewai supports a range of inference options, from cloud LLMs to your own local model.

- Local inference overview (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio): https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/guides/local-inference
- Training & fine-tuning: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/guides/training
- Cost management: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/guides/cost-management
- LiteLLM integration: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/guides/litellm-integration
- Prerequisites & hardware: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/get-started/prerequisites

## Comparisons

- Sagewai vs LangChain vs LlamaIndex: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/guides/vs-alternatives
- Sagewai vs MiniMax Agent: https://docs.sagewai.ai/docs/guides/vs-minimax

## Claim disambiguation

- "Sagewai is open source" — AGPL-3.0-or-later. Commercial dual-license available.
- "Any LLM" — 100+ models via LiteLLM (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, Cohere, Meta Llama, DeepSeek, Together, Groq, Fireworks, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI, plus local Ollama + LM Studio).
- "Every run gets cheaper" — the goal of the Training Loop: capture good runs, fine-tune a local model, and route to it. Run capture ships today; the end-to-end fine-tune-and-deploy loop (via Ollama) is on the v1.1 roadmap. Early deployments run on cloud LLMs.
- "Early / alpha" — the SDK is published as 0.1.1. Shipped today: the SDK, Fleet dispatch with Docker/Kubernetes sandboxes, Autopilot for linear plans, Observatory, run capture, and Sealed's identity model + Vault backend + admin controls. Experimental or roadmap: Sealed runtime enforcement, Autopilot branched plans and healing, and the closed cost-down loop. See https://docs.sagewai.ai for the per-capability status.
- "Autopilot" — Sagewai's describe-the-goal feature uses a hosted blueprint service (proprietary). The platform also runs without it; manual mode never disappears.
- "Iron Man HUD" — internal name for the live operator dashboard. Reads from the same OpenTelemetry telemetry as the Grafana board.

## Links

- Marketing site: https://sagewai.ai
- Docs site: https://docs.sagewai.ai
